codeReview.summary: The feature correctly broadens dashboard state derivation to show `signing-blocked` when `readiness.sign_ready` is false and adds the Trigger Sync UI wiring, but nonce-pool detection is too broad and can incorrectly show the nonce exhaustion overlay when signing is blocked by policy rather than nonce depletion.

issues.description: `isNoncePoolDepleted` currently returns true whenever `!readiness.sign_ready` and `onlinePeerCount >= readiness.threshold`, which does not isolate nonce exhaustion. In the runtime, `sign_ready` is derived from peers that are both `can_sign` and policy-allowed (`effective_policy.request.sign`), so policy-denied peers can produce `sign_ready=false` without nonce depletion. This causes false-positive 'Syncing nonces' / 'Trigger Sync' overlays and violates the requirement that this affordance appear for nonce-pool exhaustion signals specifically.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance says known pre-existing issues must not be re-reported in handoff `discoveredIssues`, but this handoff still re-reported the tracked nested-button issue.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md:93-95 explicitly says not to re-report known issues; handoff file `...m1-signing-blocked-and-nonce-overlay__9276da49-6ed1-43b0-a4b7-69c585774bc3.json`:110-114 includes the known nested `<button>` issue again.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-worker skill requires manual agent-browser verification with recorded `interactiveChecks`, but this feature handoff/transcript does not include that verification artifact.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-worker/SKILL.md:49 requires agent-browser checks for fulfilled assertions; handoff has no `interactiveChecks` section, and transcript skeleton entry (`worker-transcripts.jsonl`:7) shows no `Skill {"skill":"agent-browser"}` invocation.

summary: Reviewed feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, runtime-worker skill, and commit 342a5e1 diff. Found one blocking correctness issue: nonce depletion detection can misclassify policy-gated sign unavailability and incorrectly show Trigger Sync. Also recorded two shared-state observations about known-issue re-reporting and missing agent-browser verification artifacts.
